Petition Of Contestant University Of California San Francisco Foundation As To Subpoenaed Records Of Adult Protective Services; Stipulation And Proposed Order
Off Calendar Dec-21-2007 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Ed Kaplowitz for the Kramer Family. (Conrad Donner and Stephen Kramer also present in court). Terrence Howzell for the City and County of San Francisco (Adult Protective Services). Michelle Trausch for the UCSF Foundation. Bruce Feder for the Jewish Family Childrens Services (Special Administrator).

Ms. Trausch reported that notice had been given to Mr. Cullem. In addition, Ms. Trausch requested the stipulation for subpeonaed records to be delivered to the Court be signed, stated that the oppostion was not timely, that they were looking for evidence of the decedent's testament capacity and the decedent's realtionship with family and her client (UCSF). Mr. Kaplowitz argued that the opposition was timely because the matter was continued, that a discovery matter should be presented to the discovery department, and that this was not the proper court for this request. In addition, he stated that the records were confidential, that there was no conservatorship pending, and that there was no basis for the court to request the records. Mr. Howzell expressed that he was not authorized to produce the documents to either side. Oral argument was presented by both sides with regard to the Motion For Temporary Release Of Original Documents. The Court took both matters under submission.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Arlene Howell CSR#2542. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy Lee and Deputy Smith.

Off Calendar Apr-10-2007 Continued To May-23-07 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Edward A Koplowitz and Conrad Donner appearing for and with Katelina Dresner and Steven Kremer. Steven Kremer is the Special Administrator. Michele Trausch appearing for UCSF Foundation. Eric Behreno, general counsel for UCSF Foundation is in court. Charles Holly from Northern Trust is in court. Bruce Feder appearing for JFCS as conservator for Ms. Barrett. Jill Rudee from JFCS is in court. Issue on who should be appointed as the special administrator argued. Matter submitted. Status on will contest is set on 5/23.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
Off Calendar Feb-27-2007 Continued To Mar-26-07 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Bruce Feder appearing for conservator JFCS. Jill Rudee from JFCS is in court. Edward Koplowitz and Conrad Donner appearing for and with Special Administrator Steven Kremer. Sandy Kremer is in court. David Baer appearing for University of California San Francisco Foundation. Two representatives from Northern Trust Company are in court. Court disclosed that he knew Ms. Barrett. She was a friend of his mother-in-law. Mr. Baer waived any conflict issue. UCSF has filed a petition for letters of administration and hearing is set on 3/26. An exparte petition for appointment of special administration is set for today. Mr. Koplowitz argued that Mr. Kremer was appointed over the objection of UCSF. His appointment should be extended to 3/36. Oral argument presented by Mr. Baer for appointment of a neutral party. Court is extending the Special Letters to 3/26. He does not see any need to change at this time. Mr. Donner is requesting for additional powers to get records from Smith Barney. Mr. Baer agreed to the additional powers. Inventory issue in the conservatorship discussed. Mr. Feder has communicated with an attorney in Texas regarding the Texas property. Matter is continued to 3/26.
Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Katy Bersamin CSR#11722. Clerk: Ella Yip. Bailiff: J. Peralta.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
Off Calendar Feb-13-2007 Continued To Feb-27-07 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Conrad Donner (Counsel for Steven Kremer, Personal Representative).

Mr. Kremer appeared for purposes of continuing the hearing on this matter to February 27, 2007 at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204 which request was granted. Mr. Donner is to prepare the Order extending the existing Letters on the appropriate Judicial Council form.

Judicial Office: Stella Pantazis, Judge Pro Tempore; Reporter: David Davenport CSR #1694; Bailiff: Deputy Jimmy Peralta; Clerk: R. Michael Diles; Court Examiner: Christine Nahnson.
